items.title: Write a clear, scoped PR title
items.rule: PR titles must follow the pattern: <scope>: <concise change summary>. Keep it under 72 characters and avoid vague words like 'stuff' or 'minor tweaks'.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Consistent, scannable titles speed up triage, changelogs, and search.

items.title: Provide a complete PR description template
items.rule: Include Motivation, Approach, Considered Alternatives, Risk & Rollout, and Testing Evidence. Use bullet points; avoid single-line descriptions.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Structured context reduces back-and-forth and prevents misinterpretation.

items.title: Link an issue or ticket reference
items.rule: Reference at least one tracking ID (e.g., JIRA/GitHub issue) in the PR description using a consistent format (e.g., 'Refs: ABC-123').
items.whyIsThisImportant: Traceability ties changes to requirements and makes auditing simpler.

items.title: Right-size the PR or justify size
items.rule: If the PR is large (many files/lines), split it into logical chunks or provide a clear justification and review guide listing file groups and what to focus on.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Smaller, focused PRs speed reviews and reduce defects.

examples.snippet: Review guide section with file clusters

items.title: Avoid mixed concerns in one PR
items.rule: Do not combine mechanical changes (formatting, renames) with behavioral changes. If unavoidable, separate commits and call out which commits are mechanical.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Mixed concerns hide logic changes and slow reviews.

items.title: Include testing evidence or steps-to-reproduce
items.rule: Provide explicit verification steps (manual or automated), expected vs actual outcomes, and attach any logs or screenshots relevant to the changes.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reproducible verification accelerates review and catch regressions.

items.title: Attach visual diffs for UI changes
items.rule: When altering UI, include before/after screenshots or a short clip demonstrating the change, and note responsive states and accessibility impacts.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Visual evidence clarifies intent and reduces UI regressions.

items.title: Document rollout, flags, and rollback
items.rule: If the change is risky or user-facing, describe the feature flag, rollout plan (stages/segments), monitoring signals, and a clear rollback procedure.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Safe rollout reduces incident blast radius and MTTR.

items.title: Call out breaking changes explicitly
items.rule: If an API/contract or behavior is breaking, add a 'BREAKING CHANGE' section detailing what breaks, migration steps, and affected consumers.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Explicit warnings prevent downstream failures and accelerate coordination.

items.title: Remove dead code and noisy diffs
items.rule: Do not include commented-out code, unused files, or unrelated snapshots. Keep diffs minimal and relevant to the change.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Clean diffs improve focus, reduce repo bloat, and avoid confusion.

examples.snippet: Separate gist/ADR for historical context; diff is minimal

items.title: Update docs and changelog when user-facing
items.rule: If the change affects users or operators, update end-user docs/runbooks and add a concise changelog entry in the PR description or designated file.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Docs keep teams aligned and reduce support load.

items.title: Assign owners and request targeted reviewers
items.rule: Tag code owners or subject-matter experts for impacted areas. Include a short 'What to review' section to guide each reviewer.
items.whyIsThisImportant: The right reviewers find the right issues faster.

items.title: Add a reviewer checklist in the PR
items.rule: Provide a short checklist tailored to this change (e.g., 'logic matches spec', 'docs updated', 'flags in place', 'rollback plan noted').
items.whyIsThisImportant: Checklists reduce omissions and standardize quality gates.

items.title: Keep 'use client' at the leaf: default to Server Components
items.rule: Prefer Server Components for pages/layouts and move interactivity to small leaf Client Components. Only add 'use client' where browser APIs or event handlers are strictly necessary.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces bundle size and hydration, improves Web Vitals, and simplifies server-side data fetching.

examples.snippet: 'use client' at the top of app/(marketing)/page.tsx
Server page + small client child like <LikeButton />

items.title: Use next/image with explicit dimensions and alt
items.rule: Always use the Next.js Image component for images with width/height (or fill) and meaningful alt text. Avoid plain for app assets.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Ensures optimization, stable layout (no CLS), and responsive loading.

items.title: Prefer next/link for internal navigation
items.rule: Use <Link> from 'next/link' for internal routes; reserve <a> only for external URLs or downloadable assets.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Enables client-side navigation, controlled prefetch, and better UX.

items.title: Make fetch caching explicit in Server Components
items.rule: For dynamic data use { cache: 'no-store' } or file-level dynamic = 'force-dynamic'. For ISR use { next: { revalidate: N, tags: [...] } }.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents unexpected caching and makes SSR vs ISR strategy explicit.

items.title: Revalidate after mutations to keep UI cache coherent
items.rule: After server-side mutations, call revalidatePath or revalidateTag to refresh cached RSC data.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents stale UI after POST/PUT/DELETE.

items.title: Use Route Handlers in app/api with NextResponse
items.rule: Prefer app/api route handlers over legacy pages/api. Return NextResponse.json, validate input, and handle allowed HTTP methods explicitly.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Integrates with RSC, cache tags, and Edge runtime.

items.title: Never expose secrets to the client
items.rule: Read secrets (process.env.*) only in Server Components, Route Handlers, or server actions. Client Components must use ONLY NEXT_PUBLIC_* variables.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Protects credentials and prevents leaks in the client bundle.

items.title: Use Metadata API instead of <Head>
items.rule: Declare SEO metadata via export const metadata or generateMetadata; avoid manual <Head> in App Router.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Provides consistent, typed SEO that works with RSC.

items.title: Provide loading.tsx and error.tsx per segment
items.rule: For async routes, add loading.tsx for suspense fallbacks and error.tsx for boundaries. Use notFound() for 404 states.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves UX and isolates failures per route segment.

items.title: Use generateStaticParams for SSG on dynamic routes
items.rule: When statically prebuilding dynamic segments, implement generateStaticParams and validate params; fall back to notFound() for invalid slugs.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Brings SSG performance with safe fallbacks.

items.title: Dynamic import for heavy client-only widgets
items.rule: Use next/dynamic with { ssr: false } for large client-only components (maps, editors) and provide a lightweight loading fallback.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids SSR cost and reduces JS sent to the client.

examples.snippet: Static import of a heavy editor

items.title: Read headers/cookies only on the server
items.rule: Use headers() and cookies() in Server Components, Route Handlers, or server actions; never in Client Components.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Ensures security and compatibility with RSC.

items.title: Use next/font for fonts (no <link> to external CDNs)
items.rule: Load fonts via next/font (google or local) and apply on html/body; avoid runtime <link> to fonts that cause FOUT/CLS.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves performance and eliminates layout shifts.

items.title: Use redirect() and notFound() from next/navigation
items.rule: Prefer redirect() and notFound() over manual 302/404 handling and custom components in App Router.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Integrates with the router and simplifies control flow.

items.title: Use next/script safely and avoid inline scripts
items.rule: For third-party scripts, use <Script> with an appropriate strategy and onLoad callbacks. Avoid dangerouslySetInnerHTML for scripts.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Controls priority, reduces blocking, and improves security/CSP.

items.title: Middleware only for light auth/rewrites
items.rule: Keep middleware fast and side-effect free (no DB writes). Narrow the matcher and skip static assets. Use Route Handlers for heavy logic.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Middleware runs on many requests; unnecessary work multiplies cost.

examples.snippet: matcher that skips assets and health

items.title: Validate inputs on the server (zod) in Route Handlers/Actions
items.rule: Validate and sanitize all request bodies and search params on the server using a schema (e.g., zod). Reject invalid payloads with proper status.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents XSS/SSR injection and enforces stable API contracts.

items.title: Internationalize user-facing text with next-intl or next-i18next
items.rule: Do not hardcode user-visible strings in pages/layouts. Use an i18n library integrated with App Router and load dictionaries on the server.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Enables scalable languages, regional SEO, and consistent UX.

examples.snippet: Load dictionary on the server and render

items.title: Model mutations with Server Actions when feasible
items.rule: For simple form submissions from RSC, use server actions with schema validation and then revalidate paths/tags. Avoid client fetch for trivial mutations.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces client code, improves DX, and keeps cache consistent.

examples.snippet: Client-side fetch to create a simple item

items.title: Handle async operations with proper error handling
items.rule: Every awaited async operation must be guarded with error handling. Use try/catch for await and .catch for promise chains; never leave rejections unhandled.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Uncaught rejections cause silent failures and inconsistent states. Explicit handling makes failures observable and recoverable.

examples.snippet: try { const r = await fetch(url) } catch (e) { logger.error(e) }

items.title: Add null checks before accessing properties
items.rule: When accessing properties that may be absent, use optional chaining and sensible defaults.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents runtime TypeError crashes and makes fallback behavior explicit.

items.title: Validate environment/config at startup
items.rule: At process start, validate required configuration keys and fail fast with a clear message if any are missing or malformed.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Catches misconfiguration early and standardizes configuration contracts.

items.title: Return proper HTTP status codes for errors
items.rule: Handlers must return non-2xx status codes for error outcomes and include a minimal error body that does not leak sensitive details.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Accurate status codes make client behavior predictable and improve monitoring.

items.title: Include error context in structured logs
items.rule: Error logs must include the operation name and relevant identifiers as structured fields, not just a message string.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Context accelerates root-cause analysis and event correlation.

items.title: Re-throw or return errors to propagate failures
items.rule: Never swallow exceptions in catch blocks; either rethrow or return an explicit error result.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Hidden failures lead to data corruption and unpredictable state.

items.title: Provide error handlers to subscription/listener APIs
items.rule: When subscribing to streams/listeners, always provide an error handler and a deterministic unsubscribe/cleanup path.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents silent drops and resource leaks.

examples.snippet: ws.onmessage = onMsg; ws.onerror = onErr; ws.onclose = onClose

items.title: Classify HTTP errors with type-safe narrowing
items.rule: Define a discriminated union or error class hierarchy and use user-defined type guards before handling specific cases.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Precise handling reduces brittle branches and unsafe casts.

items.title: Log errors instead of failing silently
items.rule: Every catch block must log or report the error at least once before handling or rethrowing.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Guarantees minimum observability of failures.

items.title: Extract duplicated logic into functions
items.rule: Duplicate sequences of statements must be extracted into a named function or utility.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces maintenance cost and prevents logical drift.

items.title: Consolidate duplicated schemas
items.rule: Avoid maintaining multiple equivalent validators for the same shape; centralize checks in a single function/schema.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents drift between validation points and types.

examples.snippet: isUser1 and isUser2 with identical logic

items.title: Clear timers on teardown/unmount
items.rule: Whenever you start an interval/timeout, also define a deterministic cleanup path that clears it.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents memory leaks and background work after scope end.

items.title: Prefer responsive over fixed dimensions
items.rule: Avoid fixed pixel heights for containers; prefer min/max constraints or flexible layouts.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves accessibility and adaptability across devices.

items.title: Debounce or throttle user input that triggers work
items.rule: Apply debounce/throttle to handlers that trigger network calls or heavy computation on rapid input.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents UI jank and rate-limit issues.

items.title: Avoid expensive animations on non-composited properties
items.rule: Animate transform/opacity; avoid top/left/width/box-shadow on large elements.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Uses GPU compositing and avoids costly relayout/paint.

items.title: Use computed/derived properties for repeated calculations
items.rule: Compute expensive derived values once per input set and reuse them rather than recalculating in multiple places.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces duplicate work and logic drift.

examples.snippet: recompute the same reduce several times

items.title: Optimize chained array operations
items.rule: Prefer a single pass when possible (e.g., reduce) over multiple filter/map chains on the same array.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Cuts allocations and improves throughput on hot paths.

items.title: Hoist or cache expensive computations
items.rule: Hoist constant work (e.g., RegExp, static JSON, config loads) out of loops and cache results by input when feasible.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids unnecessary CPU and GC pressure.

examples.snippet: declare the regex once outside the loop

items.title: Memoize components and callbacks when props are stable
items.rule: For pure functions called repeatedly with the same inputs, memoize results; avoid recreating identical callbacks in hot paths.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces redundant computation and churn.

items.title: Detect N+1 style queries and suggest batching
items.rule: Flag patterns that issue network/database calls inside loops; recommend batching (Promise.all), joins, or aggregate endpoints.
items.whyIsThisImportant: N+1 patterns explode latency and load.

items.title: Derive TypeScript types from validation schemas
items.rule: Avoid duplicating runtime schemas and TypeScript types; derive types from constants/functions or declare as const tuples.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Keeps types and runtime in sync, reducing drift.

examples.snippet: const ARR=['a','b'] as const; type X=typeof ARR[number]

items.title: Prefer specific types over any/unknown where inferable
items.rule: Replace any/unknown with inferred generics, unions, or precise interfaces when the shape is known.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Preserves type safety and enables better tooling.

items.title: Use Promise.allSettled for batch operations with partial failures
items.rule: For many independent tasks, prefer Promise.allSettled and handle per-item results.
items.whyIsThisImportant: One failure should not mask other successes.

items.title: Avoid deprecated APIs via types/@deprecated metadata
items.rule: Flag usages marked as @deprecated (via JSDoc or types) and propose the documented alternative when available.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents regressions and eases dependency upgrades.

items.title: Create shared translation accessors/hooks instead of prop drilling
items.rule: Avoid prop drilling translation functions/objects; expose shared accessors (e.g., a global translation service or context API).
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces coupling across UI layers and makes i18n reusable.

examples.snippet: pass t through multiple component levels
use a shared i18n accessor instead of drilling

items.title: Replace print statements with logging framework
items.rule: Use the standard logging module (or your app's logger) instead of print() in committed code.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Structured logging supports levels, handlers, correlation IDs, and can be filtered/aggregated in production.

items.title: Remove unused imports and variables
items.rule: Delete imports and locals that are not referenced; prefer tooling (ruff/flake8/mypy) to enforce.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces noise and cognitive load, prevents accidental side effects and speeds up tooling.

items.title: Add comprehensive type hints
items.rule: Annotate function parameters and return types; use TypedDict/Protocol/Dataclass/Pydantic where appropriate.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Type hints prevent whole classes of bugs and improve IDE/refactor support.

items.title: Use timezone-aware datetime objects
items.rule: Store and compare datetimes as timezone-aware (UTC). Convert to local time only at the edges (UI/IO).
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids subtle bugs with DST/offset math and makes comparisons reliable.

items.title: Fix variable shadowing in nested loops
items.rule: Use distinct variable names across scopes; never reuse the outer loop variable in inner loops or comprehensions.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Shadowing hides bugs and makes code hard to reason about.

examples.snippet: [i for i in items for i in i.children]
[(p, c) for p in items for c in p.children]

items.title: Add specific exception handling
items.rule: Catch and handle only the exceptions you expect (e.g., KeyError, ValueError, TimeoutError); let unknown ones bubble.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents masking real failures and supports targeted recovery.

items.title: Add transaction handling with rollback
items.rule: Wrap multi-statement DB operations in a transaction; commit on success and rollback on any exception.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Guarantees atomicity and consistent data on failures.

items.title: Handle file operations errors
items.rule: Guard file IO with try/except; handle OSError and data parse errors with clear messages.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents crashes when files are missing, locked, or malformed.

examples.snippet: with open(path) as f: ...;  # try/except around

items.title: Add error handling for external service calls
items.rule: Wrap HTTP/SDK calls with timeouts and catch transport/errors; log context and map to domain failures.
items.whyIsThisImportant: External calls are unreliable; explicit handling avoids hanging and unclear states.

items.title: Use dict.get() with defaults for safe key access
items.rule: When keys may be absent, prefer dict.get(key, default) or setdefault instead of direct indexing.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids KeyError and documents fallback behavior.

items.title: Add input validation for critical parameters
items.rule: Validate presence, type, and range of critical inputs at boundaries (API, CLI, jobs) and fail fast with clear messages.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces downstream errors and improves error messages for callers.

examples.snippet: if not p: raise ValueError('p required')

items.title: Add database connection decorators
items.rule: Provide a decorator/context manager that opens/closes DB sessions and handles commit/rollback automatically.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents connection leaks and ensures consistent transaction semantics.

items.title: Add database indexes for query performance
items.rule: Identify hot lookups/joins/sorts and add appropriate single/compound indexes in migrations.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents full scans and reduces latency under load.

items.title: Optimize client initialization patterns
items.rule: Instantiate expensive clients (DB/HTTP/SDK) once and inject/reuse them; avoid per-call construction.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces connection churn and improves throughput.

items.title: Cache expensive computations
items.rule: Apply memoization (functools.lru_cache) or explicit caches for pure, expensive computations keyed by inputs.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids repeated CPU/network work on hot paths.

items.title: Optimize database operations with batching
items.rule: Use executemany/bulk operations and commit in sensible batches for large writes.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces round trips and lock contention; improves throughput.

items.title: Use list comprehensions and avoid redundant sorting
items.rule: Prefer comprehensions over map/filter chains and avoid double-sorting or sorting inside loops.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and avoids unnecessary allocations/time.

items.title: Extract duplicated logic to helper functions
items.rule: Move repeated sequences of operations into named helpers or utilities and reuse them.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Single source of truth reduces bugs and accelerates changes.

items.title: Consolidate repetitive configuration patterns
items.rule: Replace repeated set/get config calls with data-driven loops or loaders from a single schema/source.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Lowers boilerplate and keeps config behavior consistent.

items.title: Extract constants from hardcoded values
items.rule: Move magic values (strings, thresholds, paths) to module-level constants with descriptive names.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and reduces drift across call sites.

items.title: Refactor conditional logic into loops
items.rule: Replace repeated similar if/elif blocks with data-driven loops or dispatch tables.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces code size and avoids missed branches when adding cases.

items.title: Move large templates to external files
items.rule: Store long static templates/prompts in files and load them at runtime; avoid embedding huge multi-line strings in code.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and allows independent edits and caching.

items.title: Replace hardcoded paths with configuration
items.rule: Derive paths/URLs from configuration or environment variables, not string literals in code.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Enables environment-specific deployments and safer secrets management.

items.title: Make module-level dictionaries immutable
items.rule: Expose constant dicts as read-only via MappingProxyType (or frozen dataclasses) to prevent runtime mutation.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Protects shared configuration/state against accidental writes.

items.title: Use aliased imports to avoid naming collisions
items.rule: Alias imports when names overlap with locals or other modules; keep clear, unique identifiers.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids accidental shadowing and improves readability.

items.title: Replace lambda with regular functions
items.rule: Prefer named def functions for non-trivial logic; keep lambdas only for tiny, inline expressions.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Named functions are reusable, testable, and easier to log/profile.

examples.snippet: def heavy_key(x): return heavy(x); map(heavy_key, xs)

items.title: Add proper logging for audit and debugging
items.rule: Log key operations with structured fields (actor/resource/operation) at appropriate levels; include exception info on failures.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Enables traceability and forensics while keeping logs actionable.

items.title: Make verbose logging conditional
items.rule: Guard expensive debug log formatting behind level checks or lazy formatting.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids overhead in hot paths when debug logs are disabled.

items.title: Move environment configuration to startup
items.rule: Load and validate environment/config once during startup; inject settings into modules instead of re-reading env.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Ensures consistency and surfaces misconfiguration early.

items.title: Use dependency injection for resource management
items.rule: Pass dependencies (clients, sessions, configs) into functions/classes instead of creating them internally.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves testability, reuse, and separation of concerns.

items.title: Avoid modifying input parameters
items.rule: Treat function inputs as immutable; copy before mutation (e.g., dict.copy(), list(x)).
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents surprising side effects and makes functions safer to reuse.

examples.snippet: def f(xs): ys = list(xs); ys.append(1); return ys

items.title: Add unique constraints for data integrity
items.rule: Enforce uniqueness at the database level (unique indexes/constraints) for identifiers and natural keys.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Application checks are race-prone; DB constraints are authoritative and fast.

examples.snippet: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_users_email ON users(email)

items.title: Add error handling for JSON marshalling
items.rule: Always check and handle errors from json.Marshal/json.Unmarshal (or Encoder/Decoder). Log context and return a safe, consistent error to callers.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Marshalling can fail for unsupported types or invalid data, leading to empty responses or panics if ignored.

examples.snippet: b, err := json.Marshal(x); if err != nil { return err }

items.title: Return zero values on error for clarity
items.rule: When returning (T, error), return the zero value for T (or nil for pointers/slices/maps) whenever error != nil.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents accidental use of partial data and clarifies that the result is invalid when an error occurs.

examples.snippet: return result, err // result is non-zero
var z T; return z, err

items.title: Avoid exposing internal IDs in error messages
items.rule: Do not include internal identifiers (DB IDs, service IDs, tokens) in client-facing errors. Log them internally; return generic messages externally.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces information leakage, improves security, and keeps public errors stable.

items.title: Refactor duplicated validation logic
items.rule: Centralize repeated validation rules into helpers or small packages. Reuse them across handlers, services, and jobs.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents divergence of business rules and reduces maintenance cost.

examples.snippet: // copy-paste the same checks in multiple files

items.title: Extract duplicated request validation to helper functions
items.rule: For HTTP/RPC handlers, parse+validate inputs in dedicated functions. Return typed errors and reuse across endpoints.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Keeps handlers small, consistent, and easier to test.

examples.snippet: r.FormValue(...) checks repeated in each handler

items.title: Move startup validation out of interceptor factories
items.rule: Perform one-time configuration/dependency validation at process startup (init/bootstrap). Avoid doing it inside per-request middleware/interceptor factories.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents repeated work on hot paths and fails fast when configuration is invalid.

examples.snippet: validate inside middleware factory on each wiring
validate once at startup; construct middleware after

items.title: Fix goroutine execution to run in parallel
items.rule: Launch goroutines outside of waits, capture loop variables, and use sync.WaitGroup (and buffered channels when needed) to achieve true concurrency.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents accidental serialization and data races from captured variables.

examples.snippet: go func(){ use(u) }() // without capturing u

items.title: Add request context to error logs
items.rule: Include request-scoped fields (method, path, request_id, deadline/canceled) in error logs. Pass context through layers.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves debuggability and traceability across services.

items.title: Use standard HTTP status text for errors
items.rule: Return appropriate status codes and prefer http.StatusText(status) (or a consistent JSON error schema) rather than inventing custom reason phrases.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Keeps responses predictable for clients and aligns with platform conventions.

items.title: Order validations before database queries
items.rule: Validate inputs and preconditions up-front and return early before issuing any database queries.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Failing fast reduces wasted I/O, keeps code paths simple, and prevents unnecessary transaction/connection usage.

examples.snippet: if(!IsValid(id)) return; var row = await ctx.Table.FindAsync(id);

items.title: Add null checks to prevent NullReferenceException
items.rule: Guard all potentially-null references with null checks, null-coalescing, or pattern matching before dereferencing.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents runtime crashes and clarifies intended fallback behavior.

items.title: Use specific exception types over generic Exception
items.rule: Throw and catch the most specific exception type available; avoid catching or throwing System.Exception unless rethrowing.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Specific types enable precise handling, better logging, and safer retries.

items.title: Add try-catch blocks for external calls
items.rule: Wrap network, file system, and other external calls in try/catch; add context and map to application-level errors.
items.whyIsThisImportant: External dependencies fail unpredictably; explicit handling makes failures observable and recoverable.

examples.snippet: try { await client.GetAsync(u); } catch(HttpRequestException e) { /* log */ }

items.title: Await async operations properly
items.rule: Await Tasks instead of blocking (no .Result or .Wait()); prefer async/await end-to-end and configure awaits appropriately.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents deadlocks, thread pool starvation, and lost exceptions.

items.title: Use safe type casting with as operator
items.rule: Use the as operator or pattern matching for safe casts and guard null results before usage.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents InvalidCastException and makes intent explicit.

examples.snippet: if(obj is Foo foo){ /* use foo */ }

items.title: Use TryParse for string conversions
items.rule: Prefer TryParse-style APIs for user/IO input instead of Parse, and validate culture/format where applicable.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids exceptions as control flow and handles invalid input gracefully.

items.title: Extract duplicated business logic
items.rule: Move repeated domain rules into reusable methods/services; keep controllers and handlers thin.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Single source of truth improves correctness and testability.

items.title: Centralize string constants
items.rule: Define shared string literals (route names, keys, claim types) as constants/enums in a single place.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents drift and typos across the codebase.

items.title: Extract common query logic
items.rule: Factor repeated LINQ predicates/projections into reusable expressions or methods.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and ensures consistent filtering/mapping.

items.title: Refactor large methods into smaller functions
items.rule: Split long methods into cohesive private helpers with clear names and single responsibilities.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability, testability, and reduces bug surface.

items.title: Remove debug code from production
items.rule: Delete Console.WriteLine, Debug.WriteLine, and temporary diagnostics before merging; rely on structured logging.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents noisy logs and leaking internals to users.

items.title: Use proper naming conventions
items.rule: Follow .NET naming: PascalCase for types/methods/properties, camelCase for locals/parameters, and meaningful names.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Consistency improves readability and tooling support.

items.title: Use enums instead of magic strings
items.rule: Replace string literals that represent finite sets with enums (optionally flagged) or constants.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves type safety and discoverability.

items.title: Optimize database queries with JOINs
items.rule: Prefer JOINs/eager loading over per-row queries to avoid N+1 patterns.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces round-trips and improves query performance.

items.title: Use Dictionary lookups instead of linear searches
items.rule: For frequent key lookups, use Dictionary/ConcurrentDictionary instead of repeated linear scans.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves lookup performance from O(n) to O(1) average.

items.title: Cache expensive operations outside loops
items.rule: Hoist constant work out of loops and memoize expensive computations when inputs repeat.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces CPU and allocation pressure.

items.title: Use HashSet for fast membership checks
items.rule: Use HashSet<T> for repeated contains checks on large collections.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Provides O(1) average membership checks vs O(n) scans.

items.title: Use StringBuilder for string concatenation
items.rule: Use StringBuilder for many concatenations in loops or when building large strings.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids repeated allocations from immutable string concatenation.

items.title: Optimize LINQ queries for database operations
items.rule: Ensure LINQ queries translate to SQL efficiently (avoid client evaluation, use projections, defer ToList until needed).
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents loading excess data and running logic client-side.

items.title: Replace magic numbers with named constants
items.rule: Extract numeric literals with meaning into named constants or readonly fields.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and eases coordinated changes.

items.title: Fix file-to-class name mismatches
items.rule: Keep public types in files with matching names (one top-level type per file) to improve navigation.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Aids discoverability and reduces confusion for tooling and readers.

items.title: Add database indexes for query optimization
items.rule: Index frequently filtered or joined columns and verify with query plans.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Proper indexing drastically improves query performance and reduces timeouts.

items.title: Optimize string column types
items.rule: Use appropriate column types/lengths (e.g., NVARCHAR(128) vs NVARCHAR(MAX)) for fields with known bounds.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Smaller types improve storage, index efficiency, and query speed.

items.title: Avoid empty catch blocks
items.rule: Never swallow exceptions silently; log with context and either rethrow or handle explicitly.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Silent failures are hard to diagnose and can corrupt state.

items.title: Check query results before accessing indices
items.rule: Validate collection bounds and query results before indexing; prefer FirstOrDefault/SingleOrDefault with null checks.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents IndexOutOfRangeException and null dereferences.

items.title: Handle transaction rollbacks properly
items.rule: Wrap multi-step writes in a transaction; on failure, roll back and rethrow with context.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Maintains data consistency under partial failures.

examples.snippet: SaveChanges called multiple times w/o transaction

items.title: Separate UI logic from business logic
items.rule: Keep UI/controllers thin; move domain logic to services or domain objects.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves testability, reuse, and separation of concerns.

items.title: Use dependency injection over direct instantiation
items.rule: Request dependencies via constructors and register them with the DI container; avoid new-ing services in code.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Enables testing, configuration, and lifecycle management.

items.title: Return DTOs instead of domain entities
items.rule: Expose DTOs/ViewModels from API endpoints; avoid leaking persistence/domain entities directly.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Encapsulates internals and stabilizes public contracts.

items.title: Use appropriate HTTP status codes
items.rule: Return 2xx on success, 4xx for client errors, and 5xx for server errors with minimal, non-sensitive bodies.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Correct semantics enable predictable client behavior and monitoring.

items.title: Group related parameters in objects
items.rule: Replace long parameter lists with request objects or records; validate them as a unit.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability, evolution, and validation.

items.title: Use private access modifiers for encapsulation
items.rule: Prefer the most restrictive access modifier that works (private/internal) and expose only necessary members.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces surface area and accidental coupling.

items.title: Initialize properties with default values
items.rule: Set sensible defaults for properties/fields or enforce via constructors.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents null/invalid states and clarifies invariants.

items.title: Remove unreachable code
items.rule: Delete dead code and after-return/throw statements that never execute.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces noise and avoids misleading readers and analyzers.

items.title: Use using statements for disposable resources
items.rule: Wrap IDisposable resources in using/await using to ensure deterministic disposal.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents leaks of streams, handles, and sockets.

items.title: Use IReadOnlyList for immutable collections
items.rule: Expose read-only views (IReadOnlyList/IReadOnlyCollection) from APIs that should not allow mutation.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Encodes intent and prevents accidental modifications.

items.title: Remove redundant LINQ calls
items.rule: Avoid unnecessary AsQueryable/ToList/Where/Select chains that do nothing; keep pipelines minimal.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces allocations and clarifies intent.

items.title: Implement proper database error checking
items.rule: Detect transient vs non-transient DB errors; add context and apply retry policies only where safe (idempotent reads/operations).
items.whyIsThisImportant: Enables safe retries, correct surfacing of validation/constraint failures, and better diagnostics.

examples.snippet: catch(DbUpdateException e){ if(IsUnique(e)) return Conflict(); throw; }

items.title: Use enum constants instead of hardcoded values
items.rule: When a value comes from a finite set, model it as an enum constant instead of string/int literals.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Enums provide type safety, discoverability, and avoid typos and drift.

items.title: Add comprehensive null checks for parameters
items.rule: Validate method inputs up front with null checks or use Objects.requireNonNull and meaningful messages.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents NullPointerException, documents preconditions, and fails fast near the source.

items.title: Replace printStackTrace with proper logging
items.rule: Do not use printStackTrace/System.err; log via a structured logger with context and level.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Structured logs are routable, parsable, and support levels/contexts for observability.

items.title: Propagate exceptions instead of hiding failures
items.rule: Never swallow exceptions silently; wrap with context and rethrow or translate to a domain exception.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Hidden failures corrupt state and break retries/alerts.

examples.snippet: catch(Exception e){ throw new RuntimeException("context", e); }

items.title: Add try-catch blocks for external service calls
items.rule: Wrap network/filesystem/process calls; check status codes/results and add context to failures.
items.whyIsThisImportant: External dependencies are unreliable; explicit handling makes issues observable and recoverable.

items.title: Use try-with-resources for resource management
items.rule: Use try-with-resources for Closeable/AutoCloseable objects to guarantee deterministic cleanup.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents resource leaks of streams/sockets and simplifies finally blocks.

items.title: Return explicit HTTP responses from API methods
items.rule: API endpoints should return an explicit status code and minimal error body on failures; avoid 200 with error payload.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Correct semantics enable predictable client handling and monitoring.

items.title: Handle empty async/reactive results with appropriate responses
items.rule: When using Optional/CompletableFuture/Publisher-like types, map empty values to a clear outcome (e.g., 404) rather than returning null.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids ambiguous nulls and clarifies API contracts.

items.title: Prefer dependency injection over manual factories
items.rule: Obtain collaborators via constructor injection (or a DI container) instead of new/service locators in business code.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves testability, configuration, and lifecycle management.

items.title: Extract duplicated API call logic
items.rule: Factor repeated HTTP call setup/parsing/retry into a reusable helper or client.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces copy-paste errors and centralizes cross-cutting concerns.

items.title: Consolidate multiple similar methods
items.rule: Merge near-duplicate methods into a single parameterized method or strategy.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Single source of truth reduces maintenance and bugs.

items.title: Extract business logic into separate methods
items.rule: Move complex domain calculations out of controllers/handlers into services or private helpers.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves cohesion, reuse, and test coverage.

items.title: Improve field encapsulation with private visibility
items.rule: Use private fields with accessors; expose the narrowest visibility that satisfies usage.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces coupling and preserves invariants.

items.title: Use specific types over overly generic ones
items.rule: Prefer precise types (long, BigDecimal, LocalDate) over Number/Object when the domain is known.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves type safety and prevents accidental misuse.

items.title: Remove unused parameters and commented code
items.rule: Delete dead/commented code and unused params; rely on version control for history.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces noise and confusion for maintainers and tools.

items.title: Use descriptive variable names
items.rule: Choose names that reflect intent; avoid abbreviations except widely accepted ones.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and reduces misinterpretation.

items.title: Use String.replace for literal replacements
items.rule: Use String#replace for literal substrings instead of replaceAll unless regex is required.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids regex overhead and escaping bugs.

items.title: Cache expensive object creations
items.rule: Hoist and reuse costly objects (Pattern, DateTimeFormatter) rather than re-creating per use.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces CPU/GCs and improves throughput.

items.title: Use static constants for inline collections
items.rule: Extract repeated inline collections to unmodifiable static constants.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and prevents accidental mutation.

items.title: Optimize database queries with proper indexing
items.rule: Index columns used in JOIN/WHERE/ORDER BY; verify plans and avoid full scans for hot queries.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Indexes drastically reduce query latency and lock contention.

items.title: Use early return patterns for control flow
items.rule: Validate and return early to reduce nesting and improve readability.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Flatter code is easier to reason about and maintain.

items.title: Optimize stream operations
items.rule: Combine filters/maps to minimize passes; avoid creating intermediate collections unnecessarily.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Fewer passes reduce allocations and CPU.

items.title: Remove redundant condition checks in loops
items.rule: Avoid re-checking loop-invariant conditions inside iterations; lift them out.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces unnecessary work and clarifies intent.

items.title: Extract query strings to constants
items.rule: Define SQL strings as static final constants or in a repository, not inline across methods.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Eases maintenance, reuse, and prevents typos.

examples.snippet: static final String SQL = "UPDATE t SET...";

items.title: Use named constants for magic values
items.rule: Replace numeric/string literals with named constants to document meaning.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and centralizes changes.

items.title: Add input validation for API parameters
items.rule: Validate presence, format, and ranges for incoming parameters before processing.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents invalid operations and provides clear feedback to clients.

items.title: Validate parameter types before casting
items.rule: Use instanceof/pattern matching and guard before casts; avoid ClassCastException.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Ensures safe handling of heterogeneous data.

items.title: Add proper error handling for date parsing
items.rule: Parse dates with DateTimeFormatter in try/catch and validate expected formats/timezones.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Input dates are error-prone; explicit handling prevents runtime crashes.

items.title: Handle asynchronous operation failures
items.rule: Attach exceptionally/handle to CompletableFuture chains; never assume success.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Ensures failures are observed and mapped to consistent outcomes.

items.title: Use non-blocking patterns for async operations
items.rule: Prefer thenCompose/thenCombine over blocking get/join; propagate async results end-to-end.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids thread starvation and improves scalability.

items.title: Review long transaction/query timeouts
items.rule: Set sensible timeouts for queries/transactions and avoid excessively long timeouts that mask problems.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents hung resources and surfaces backpressure quickly.

items.title: Clarify persistence/serialization with annotations
items.rule: Annotate fields/props to explicitly control persistence and serialization (e.g., transient/ignored fields).
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents leaking internal fields and clarifies storage behavior.

items.title: Move expensive operations outside loops
items.rule: Lift loop-invariant computations (regex, formatters, lookups) out of loops and reuse.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces repeated work and allocations.

items.title: Add error handling for file operations
items.rule: Wrap file reads/writes (e.g., file_get_contents, file_put_contents, fopen) with checks and exceptions; never assume the filesystem is available.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Filesystem calls can fail due to permissions, missing files, or I/O errors. Explicit handling prevents silent data loss and cryptic warnings.

examples.snippet: $c = file_get_contents($p); if($c===false) throw new RuntimeException('read fail');

items.title: Handle JSON encoding and decoding failures
items.rule: Use JSON_THROW_ON_ERROR (PHP ≥7.3) and wrap json_encode/json_decode in try/catch; validate the decoded shape.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Malformed payloads and encoding issues are common. Exceptions make failures explicit and prevent using partial/invalid data.

items.title: Add validation for HTTP responses
items.rule: Check transport errors and HTTP status codes before parsing response bodies; treat 4xx/5xx as errors with context.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Parsing or trusting error pages as data leads to misleading behavior and crashes.

items.title: Validate array keys before accessing
items.rule: Use isset()/array_key_exists() or null coalescing (??) before reading array keys that may be absent.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents undefined index notices and documents expected defaults.

items.title: Add default cases to match expressions
items.rule: Every match expression must define a default arm that handles unsupported or unexpected values.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents UnhandledMatchError and makes behavior explicit for future enum/constant additions.

items.title: Use try-catch blocks in batch processing
items.rule: Wrap per-item work in try/catch, collect failures, and continue when appropriate; never let one failure abort the entire batch by default.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves resilience and observability of partial failures.

items.title: Handle database transaction failures
items.rule: Use transactions for multi-step writes; on Throwable, rollback and rethrow with context.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents partial writes and keeps data consistent under failures.

items.title: Throw exceptions on job failures
items.rule: Background jobs must throw on irrecoverable failures (after logging) so the runner/queue can retry or dead-letter appropriately.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Swallowed errors make queues appear healthy while losing work.

items.title: Extract duplicated business logic
items.rule: Move repeated domain calculations/transformations into named functions or service classes; keep controllers/scripts thin.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Single source of truth reduces bugs and improves testability.

items.title: Centralize configuration constants
items.rule: Define configuration values in one place (env/config file/const class) and reference them; avoid scattering magic strings.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents drift and simplifies environment-specific changes.

items.title: Consolidate duplicated query logic
items.rule: Extract reusable SQL fragments/builders for commonly repeated WHERE/ORDER clauses.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Keeps filters consistent and reduces errors when changing criteria.

items.title: Optimize queries with eager loading (avoid N+1)
items.rule: Replace per-row lookups with JOINs or bulk queries to fetch related data in fewer round-trips.
items.whyIsThisImportant: N+1 patterns explode latency and DB load under scale.

items.title: Use lazy loading/streaming for large datasets
items.rule: Stream results (cursor/generator) or paginate instead of loading entire result sets into memory.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces memory pressure and improves throughput on big tables.

items.title: Cache expensive database operations
items.rule: Cache frequently used, rarely changing query results in memory or an external cache with appropriate TTL/invalidations.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids repeated heavy queries, improving latency and DB load.

items.title: Use batch operations for deletions/updates
items.rule: Prefer set-based SQL (DELETE/UPDATE with WHERE IN/JOIN) or chunked batches over per-row statements.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces round-trips and lock contention; improves performance.

items.title: Remove redundant database queries
items.rule: Deduplicate identical queries within a request; reuse previously fetched data or preload in a single query.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids unnecessary DB load and latency.

examples.snippet: bulk load then map by id

items.title: Use deterministic ordering in queries
items.rule: Always specify ORDER BY when the processing logic relies on a particular order.
items.whyIsThisImportant: DBs may return rows in arbitrary order; explicit ordering ensures repeatable behavior.

examples.snippet: SELECT * FROM t ORDER BY created_at DESC

items.title: Validate API response structure
items.rule: After decoding, assert required keys and types before use; reject unexpected shapes.
items.whyIsThisImportant: External APIs change. Structural checks prevent downstream errors and data corruption.

items.title: Return proper HTTP status codes
items.rule: Return 2xx only on success; use 4xx for client errors and 5xx for server errors, with a minimal JSON error body.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Correct semantics enable clients and monitors to react properly.

items.title: Include context in error logs
items.rule: Log errors with operation name and identifiers (e.g., userId, orderId) using structured context.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Contextual logs speed up incident triage and correlation.

items.title: Replace echo with proper logging
items.rule: Do not use echo/var_dump for diagnostics in application code; use a PSR-3 logger or error_log with levels.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Echo contaminates HTTP responses/CLI output and breaks APIs; logging is routable and level-aware.

items.title: Remove temporary debug code
items.rule: Eliminate debug helpers (var_dump, die, print_r) and commented-out blocks before merging.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Debug code leaks internals, breaks responses, and confuses future maintainers.

items.title: Use type hints for better clarity
items.rule: Add scalar/union/nullable type hints and return types to functions, methods, and properties; prefer interfaces over mixed.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Type hints improve readability, tooling, and runtime safety.

items.title: Handle null values with coalescing operators
items.rule: Use ?? and the nullsafe operator (?->) to provide defaults and avoid notices when values may be null.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents undefined/index notices and clarifies default behavior.

items.title: Extract duplicated field lists to constants
items.rule: When the same set of attribute/field names is used in multiple places (strong params, serializers, queries), extract it to a constant and reuse.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids drift and typos; a single edit updates all call sites.

items.title: Cache expensive method calls outside loops
items.rule: Do not recompute expensive values inside loops; compute once and reuse.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces CPU and GC pressure in hot paths.

items.title: Use update_all instead of each with update
items.rule: When callbacks/validations are not required, prefer a single UPDATE statement via update_all over iterating records and calling update.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Eliminates N+1 updates and speeds up batch changes.

items.title: Optimize queries with joins and includes
items.rule: Use joins for filtering on associations and includes/preload for eager-loading to avoid N+1.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces query count and latency.

items.title: Use find_each for large collections
items.rule: Iterate large ActiveRecord sets with find_each/find_in_batches to stream records in batches.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids loading all rows in memory and reduces timeouts.

items.title: Add database indexes for query performance
items.rule: Index columns used in WHERE/JOIN/ORDER and enforce uniqueness where applicable.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves query speed and data integrity.

items.title: Use safe navigation operator for nil checks
items.rule: Use `&.` and presence/|| to safely traverse possibly-nil chains and provide defaults.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents NoMethodError from nil and clarifies fallbacks.

items.title: Specify StandardError explicitly in rescue blocks
items.rule: Avoid bare `rescue`; rescue StandardError (or a narrower class) and log with context.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents accidentally catching system-level exceptions and improves observability.

items.title: Add error handling for API response access
items.rule: Validate HTTP status and safely access nested response data using `dig`/guards.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids method errors on unexpected payloads and surfaces transport failures.

items.title: Handle ActiveRecord::RecordNotFound specifically
items.rule: Rescue RecordNotFound and return 404 (or domain-appropriate response) instead of 500.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Communicates proper semantics to clients and avoids noisy error logs.

items.title: Add transaction blocks for data consistency
items.rule: Wrap multi-record changes in a database transaction and use bang methods to ensure rollback on failure.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Keeps data consistent when any step fails.

items.title: Handle Time.parse and Date.parse errors
items.rule: Parse dates/times in begin/rescue blocks and validate expected format/timezone.
items.whyIsThisImportant: User input and APIs often send malformed timestamps.

items.title: Protect against NoMethodError on nil objects
items.rule: Guard early or use safe navigation when dereferencing possibly-nil data.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids crashes and clarifies control flow.

items.title: Extract shared Sidekiq queue names to constants
items.rule: Centralize queue names and reuse across workers and tests.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents typos and keeps routing consistent.

items.title: Extract duplicated business logic to models
items.rule: Move domain behavior from controllers/views into models or service objects.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves cohesion, reuse, and testability.

items.title: Move generic helpers to ApplicationHelper
items.rule: Place cross-controller view helpers in ApplicationHelper (or a shared module) for reuse.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids duplication and inconsistent behavior across views.

items.title: Create base controllers for shared concerns
items.rule: Factor shared before_actions/filters/error handling into ApplicationController or a base controller.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves consistency and reduces boilerplate.

items.title: Replace hardcoded values with named constants
items.rule: Give semantic names to magic numbers/strings and freeze where appropriate.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Documents intent and centralizes change.

items.title: Use translation keys for user-facing text
items.rule: Do not hardcode UI strings; use I18n keys with proper scopes.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Enables localization and consistent copy.

items.title: Use descriptive variable names
items.rule: Prefer intent-revealing names over cryptic abbreviations.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and reduces misunderstandings.

items.title: Freeze constants to prevent modification
items.rule: Freeze constant objects (arrays/hashes/strings) to avoid accidental mutation at runtime.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Preserves invariants and prevents subtle bugs.

items.title: Use Rails conventions for resource naming
items.rule: Follow Rails conventions (plural resource routes, model singular class names, snake_case files).
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces surprise and tooling friction.

items.title: Extract complex migration logic to separate methods
items.rule: Keep migrations readable by extracting multi-step data fixes to helper methods within the migration.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Eases review and reduces errors in irreversible changes.

items.title: Use change_table for safer migrations
items.rule: Group column/index changes with change_table; use concurrent indexes when needed.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and can reduce lock duration.

items.title: Make migrations reversible with up/down methods
items.rule: Prefer reversible change or explicit up/down to support rollbacks.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Allows safe deploy/rollback workflows.

items.title: Use batch operations for large data migrations
items.rule: Process large tables with find_in_batches and avoid loading entire datasets.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents memory bloat and long locks.

items.title: Add foreign key constraints in migrations
items.rule: Enforce referential integrity with add_foreign_key and matching indexes.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents orphaned rows and improves join performance.

items.title: Use guard clauses for early returns
items.rule: Validate and return early to avoid deep nesting.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Simplifies control flow and reduces cognitive load.

items.title: Extract complex conditions to private methods
items.rule: Move long conditional expressions to well-named predicate methods.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and testability.

items.title: Use or instead of + for combining queries
items.rule: Combine ActiveRecord relations with `or` instead of materializing arrays or concatenating results.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Keeps work in the database and reduces memory usage.

items.title: Return consistent types from methods
items.rule: A method should return the same type in all code paths (e.g., always an array or always nil/record).
items.whyIsThisImportant: Simplifies callers and prevents conditional type checks.

items.title: Use Struct instead of OpenStruct
items.rule: Prefer Struct (or value objects) over OpenStruct for performance and type clarity.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Structs are faster, lighter, and define a stable API.

items.title: Memoize expensive operations
items.rule: Cache expensive pure results using memoization (`||=`) or request-scoped caches.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids repeated work across a request or loop.

items.title: Externalize URLs and API keys to configuration
items.rule: Read API URLs/keys from ENV/credentials and inject where needed; do not hardcode.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Supports multiple environments and keeps secrets out of code.

items.title: Re-raise exceptions to preserve error context
items.rule: Do not swallow errors; log with context and re-raise using `raise` (no args) or `raise new_error, cause: e`.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Preserves backtrace and supports failure propagation/retries.

items.title: Add proper retry configuration for jobs
items.rule: Configure Sidekiq retries and dead-lettering; use backoff for transient failures and idempotent jobs.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves resilience and prevents infinite failure loops.

items.title: Initialize variables to prevent NameError
items.rule: Always initialize variables before use and avoid conditionally-defined locals.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents NameError and makes control flow explicit.

items.title: Remove redundant method calls
items.rule: Avoid no-op or duplicate calls (e.g., flatten.flatten, compact followed by compact!).
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces noise and minor performance overhead.

items.title: Remove unused variables and assignments
items.rule: Delete unused locals or prefix with underscore when required by interface.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and eliminates misleading code.

items.title: Remove dead code and unused methods
items.rule: Delete unreachable branches and unused methods; rely on VCS for history.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces maintenance cost and cognitive overhead.

items.title: Remove duplicated method definitions
items.rule: Do not define the same method multiple times; consolidate behavior in one place.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids accidental overrides and confusion.

items.title: Centralize analytics tracking logic
items.rule: Route all analytics events through a single service/helper instead of calling the SDK directly from widgets and blocs.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids duplication, keeps event names/properties consistent, and makes it easy to swap providers or add privacy guards.

items.title: Extract magic numbers to named constants
items.rule: Replace raw numeric/string literals used for dimensions, durations, and thresholds with named constants.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and makes changes safer and centralized.

items.title: Wrap Uri.parse in try-catch blocks
items.rule: Use try/catch (or Uri.tryParse with validation) around Uri.parse to guard against FormatException and invalid inputs.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents crashes from malformed URLs and enables clear error reporting.

examples.snippet: final u = Uri.tryParse(input); if (u != null) open(u);

items.title: Avoid swallowing exceptions by returning null
items.rule: Do not silently catch and return null on errors; log context and either rethrow or return a typed failure/Result.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Silent failures hide defects and make debugging and retries harder.

examples.snippet: catch (e, st) { logger.severe('fail', e, st); rethrow; }

items.title: Use rethrow to preserve stack traces
items.rule: When re-emitting a caught exception, use `rethrow` instead of `throw e` to keep the original stack trace.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Original stack traces are essential for accurate debugging and error grouping.

items.title: Add error handling to app initialization
items.rule: Guard app startup with zone-level handlers and FlutterError hooks; surface fatal errors and report them.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents white-screen crashes and centralizes crash logging from the very first frame.

items.title: Handle StateError from firstWhere operations
items.rule: Always provide `orElse` to firstWhere (or use a safe helper) when the match may not exist.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids StateError at runtime and makes absence explicit.

items.title: Capture and print stack traces in catch blocks
items.rule: Use `catch (e, stack)` and log both exception and stack; do not log message-only.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Complete telemetry accelerates triage and grouping in log systems.

items.title: Add const modifiers for better performance
items.rule: Mark compile-time constants and constant widgets with `const` to reduce rebuild cost and GC pressure.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Const widgets are canonicalized and not rebuilt unnecessarily.

items.title: Add dispose methods to prevent memory leaks
items.rule: Dispose controllers, focus nodes, animations, timers, and cancel subscriptions in `dispose()`.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents background work and resource leaks after widget teardown.

examples.snippet: late final t = Timer(...); @override dispose() => t.cancel();

items.title: Remove unused imports
items.rule: Delete imports that are not referenced by the compilation unit.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces noise, compile time, and accidental side effects.

items.title: Remove unused configuration variables
items.rule: Delete dead configuration fields and env flags; keep only variables that change behavior.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids confusion and prevents stale toggles from drifting.

items.title: Remove redundant null-assertion operators
items.rule: Avoid `!` when nullability is already proven or a safer alternative (`??`, early return) exists.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces risk of unexpected late null errors and clarifies intent.

items.title: Use localized strings instead of hardcoded text
items.rule: Read UI strings from a localization source (e.g., AppLocalizations, arb) rather than hardcoding.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Enables i18n, consistent copy, and accessibility reviews.

items.title: Define reusable constants for repeated values
items.rule: Create theme-like constants (radii, gaps, durations) and reuse across widgets.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves design consistency and eases global tweaks.

items.title: Extract duplicated widget logic to reusable methods
items.rule: Factor identical widget trees or builders into private helpers or dedicated widgets.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces duplication and keeps visual behavior consistent.

items.title: Extract complex conditional logic to separate methods
items.rule: Move long/branchy UI decisions into named predicates or mappers to keep build methods simple.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and testability of control flow.

items.title: Refactor duplicated dialog action logic
items.rule: Centralize repeated dialog creation/action handlers into a helper to ensure consistent UX and error handling.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents drift in copy, button order, and analytics hooks.

items.title: Define dialog handlers once to avoid duplication
items.rule: For confirm/cancel flows, define common callbacks (confirm/cancel) once and reuse across dialogs.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces copy-paste mistakes and ensures consistent analytics/logging on actions.

items.title: Replace null assertions with explicit null handling
items.rule: Avoid the !! operator. Prefer safe calls, Elvis (?:), early returns, or explicit exceptions with a clear message.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Null assertions crash at runtime and hide intent. Explicit handling makes failure modes predictable and testable.

examples.snippet: val email = user?.email ?: throw IllegalStateException("missing email")

items.title: Use idiomatic Kotlin null-handling patterns
items.rule: Use safe calls (?.), the Elvis operator (?:), let/run/also for scoped handling, and early returns for invalid input.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Idiomatic null handling reduces branching, clarifies intent, and prevents NullPointerException.

items.title: Add comprehensive input validation
items.rule: Validate required parameters at boundaries using require/requireNotNull or explicit validators; fail fast with actionable messages.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents propagation of invalid state and reduces downstream error handling complexity.

items.title: Wrap blocking operations with proper dispatchers
items.rule: Run blocking I/O and CPU-intensive work inside withContext(Dispatchers.IO) or Dispatchers.Default respectively. Never block the main thread.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Misplaced blocking work causes ANRs, thread starvation, and throughput collapse.

items.title: Add try-catch blocks around external API calls
items.rule: Surround network and IPC calls with try/catch for IOException and timeouts; map to domain errors and log context.
items.whyIsThisImportant: External systems fail unpredictably; explicit handling avoids crashes and improves resilience.

items.title: Handle database operation exceptions
items.rule: Catch and map DB exceptions (e.g., SQLIntegrityConstraintViolationException) to domain results; always close resources with use { }.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents leaks and turns low-level failures into meaningful responses.

items.title: Add timeout configuration for external clients
items.rule: Set connect, read, and write timeouts for HTTP/DB/queue clients; never rely on indefinite waits.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Bounded time prevents resource exhaustion and cascaded failures.

items.title: Replace unsafe type casts with explicit checking
items.rule: Use safe casts (as?) with null checks or require statements instead of unchecked as.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids ClassCastException at runtime and clarifies expected types.

examples.snippet: val xs = x as? List<String> ?: emptyList()

items.title: Optimize loops with early termination
items.rule: Exit loops as soon as the goal is achieved using break/return or use any/find/count short-circuiting operations.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces unnecessary work and improves clarity.

examples.snippet: for (x in xs) { if (ok(x)) do(x) } // no break

items.title: Use map lookups instead of iteration
items.rule: Prefer direct Map access over linear scans when looking up by key.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces complexity from O(n) to O(1) average and simplifies code.

items.title: Cache expensive operations and API tokens
items.rule: Memoize expensive results (regex, serializers, tokens) and refresh on expiry using a thread-safe cache.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces latency and external rate-limit pressure.

items.title: Use periodic tasks over per-entry scheduling
items.rule: For large sets, prefer a periodic sweep job rather than spawning one job/timer per item.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents scheduler overload and simplifies failure handling.

items.title: Define constants to avoid re-creation
items.rule: Hoist constant Regex/Formatters/Collections into top-level vals or companion objects; avoid recreating in hot paths.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Cuts allocations and GC pressure.

items.title: Use atomic operations for cache initialization
items.rule: Protect one-time initializations with Mutex or atomic operations (computeIfAbsent/compareAndSet).
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents duplicate work and race conditions under concurrency.

items.title: Add database indexes for query performance
items.rule: Index columns used in filters/joins/sorts; validate plans for slow queries and add composite indexes as needed.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Eliminates full scans and reduces latency at scale.

items.title: Extract duplicated interaction handlers
items.rule: Factor common interaction/message handling into reusable functions or classes with parameters for the differing bits.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces copy-paste and keeps behavior consistent.

items.title: Consolidate duplicated permission checking logic
items.rule: Provide a single permission check API (e.g., can(user, action, resource)) and reuse it across layers.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents drift and subtle authorization bugs.

items.title: Refactor duplicated modal creation logic
items.rule: Create factories/builders for repeated dialog/modal construction with configurable parameters.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Keeps UI consistent and reduces boilerplate.

items.title: Extract common parallel processing patterns
items.rule: Encapsulate coroutine parallelization (map async/awaitAll, limited concurrency) into utilities.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids repeated, error-prone concurrency code and enforces limits.

items.title: Consolidate message update functions
items.rule: Provide a single updater that applies deltas/patches to messages rather than multiple ad-hoc updaters.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents inconsistent states and race conditions.

items.title: Extract contract processing logic
items.rule: Move domain-specific processing (validation, normalization, mapping) into cohesive services instead of scattering it across controllers/handlers.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves testability and reuse; controllers become thin.

items.title: Refactor nested flatMap chains
items.rule: Break deep map/flatMap chains into named suspend functions or use runCatching/Result for clearer composition.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and error propagation.

items.title: Extract validation logic to separate methods
items.rule: Centralize validation in dedicated functions/objects and reuse; return typed errors.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Avoids duplication and inconsistent rules.

items.title: Define hardcoded strings as shared constants
items.rule: Replace magic strings with constants or enums sealed classes; keep them in a single source of truth.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents typos and eases refactors and localization.

items.title: Use message keys for error messages
items.rule: Emit stable message keys (for i18n/observability) and resolve user-facing text via localization layers.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Enables translations and consistent client handling.

items.title: Standardize parameter naming conventions
items.rule: Use lowerCamelCase for parameters; prefer full, descriptive names over abbreviations.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves readability and API clarity.

items.title: Make properties private for encapsulation
items.rule: Default to private visibility; expose the minimal surface and prefer val with private set when mutation is needed.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces coupling and prevents accidental misuse.

items.title: Rename functions to reflect their purpose
items.rule: Function names must describe side effects and return values (e.g., fetchUser vs. process).
items.whyIsThisImportant: Improves discoverability and reduces misuses.

items.title: Add comprehensive error logging
items.rule: Log failures with operation name, key identifiers, and exception; prefer structured logging (fields).
items.whyIsThisImportant: Accelerates debugging and enables reliable alerting.

examples.snippet: logger.error("op_failed", mapOf("op" to op, "err" to e))

items.title: Log warning for unhandled cases
items.rule: In when/else or default branches, log a warning with the unexpected value to surface silent failures.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Makes edge cases visible before they become incidents.

items.title: Enhance logging with structured format
items.rule: Prefer key-value or JSON logging over string concatenation; include correlation/request IDs from CoroutineContext when available.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Structured logs are machine-parsable for search and alerting.

items.title: Centralize key generation logic
items.rule: Generate cache/Redis keys through a single utility with clear namespaces and versioning.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents collisions and simplifies migrations/invalidations.

items.title: Add cleanup mechanisms for resource management
items.rule: Ensure close/stop/dispose is called for resources (clients, channels, scopes) using use{} or try/finally; cancel coroutines on shutdown.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Prevents leaks and dangling tasks, enabling graceful shutdowns.

items.title: Pin exact versions with digests for base images
items.rule: Base images must be pinned to an exact version and content digest (@sha256) to guarantee reproducible builds and supply-chain integrity.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Tags can be retagged and drift over time. Digests lock the image content, preventing unexpected changes and reducing the blast radius of registry compromises.

items.title: Pin specific patch versions for Node.js
items.rule: Use a fully qualified Node.js tag including major.minor.patch, not floating majors/minors.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Floating tags (e.g., 20, 20-alpine) can change behavior between builds, causing non-deterministic failures.

items.title: Extract hardcoded versions to centralized configuration
items.rule: Parameterize tool/runtime versions via ARGs (and CI build args) instead of hardcoding them across multiple Dockerfiles.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Centralization prevents configuration drift and makes upgrades a single-point change.

examples.snippet: ENV PNPM_VERSION=8.15.0 # repeated in many files

ARG PNPM_VERSION
RUN corepack prepare pnpm@${PNPM_VERSION} --activate

items.title: Consolidate duplicate Dockerfiles
items.rule: Deduplicate near-identical Dockerfiles by sharing a common base stage (multi-stage) and varying only service-specific steps via build args/targets.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Reduces maintenance overhead and prevents divergent build behavior between services.

examples.snippet: # Two Dockerfiles differing only in CMD
ARG SERVICE && multi-stage targets share a common base

items.title: Implement multi-stage builds for production
items.rule: Use a builder stage for toolchains/dev deps and a minimal runtime stage that contains only runtime artifacts.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Significantly reduces image size and attack surface by excluding compilers and dev dependencies from production.

items.title: Optimize Docker layer caching strategy
items.rule: Copy and install dependencies before application sources; ensure .dockerignore excludes node_modules and build outputs.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Maximizes cache hits for dependency layers, speeding up incremental builds.

items.title: Remove unnecessary files from production images
items.rule: Ship only runtime artifacts (binaries, compiled assets, minimal configs). Exclude tests, sources, docs, and dev tooling.
items.whyIsThisImportant: Smaller images pull faster, have fewer vulnerabilities, and reduce secrets exposure.

items.title: Clean up build tools after dependency installation
